column1: She (to sleep) when you came home? — No, she (to knit).
At seven o’clock yesterday I (to go) to the theatre.
What you (to do) at 5 o’clock yesterday? — I (to play) the piano.
We (to play) in the yard the whole evening yesterday.
When I rang up my friend, he (to sleep).
Father (to watch) TV at ten o’clock yesterday.
When I came home, Kate (to play) the piano.
I (to read) a book at six o’clock yesterday.
At this time yesterday I (to watch) a play by Chekhov at the theatre.
What you (to do) at six o’clock yesterday?
I (not to wash) the dishes from six till seven yesterday.
We  (not to discuss) the latest news from three till four yesterday.
